description_long: This sculpture is a detailed representation of an ancient Egyptian sphinx, featuring the body of a reclining lion and the head of a pharaoh adorned with a nemes headdress and a false beard. The face is finely carved, with distinct eyes, nose, and lips, and the headdress includes the uraeus (cobra) symbol at the forehead. The body is muscular, with clearly defined paws and a long, straight tail curled around the right haunch. Hieroglyphic-like engravings are present on the sides of the forelegs, adding to the authenticity of the design. The sculpture rests on a rectangular base, which provides stability and a sense of monumentality. The surface appears to be made of a light-colored stone or stone-like material, with a matte, slightly rough finish.
description: A finely detailed stone sphinx sculpture with a pharaoh's head, lion's body, and hieroglyphic engravings, resting on a rectangular base. The surface is light-colored and matte, evoking ancient Egyptian artistry.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pharaoh's face, headdress, and both forepaws prominently, with the full symmetry of the sphinx's features.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the head, the length of the lion's body, and the hieroglyphic engravings on the foreleg.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the headdress, the lion's haunches, and the tail curled to the right.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the opposite profile, including the pharaoh's face, body, and additional engravings on the foreleg.
attribute_subject_matter: Ancient Egyptian sphinx with pharaoh's head and lion's body
attribute_location_context: No specific setting is visible; the sculpture appears isolated against a plain background.
